title: Vegetable Spring Rolls

overview.description: Crisp Chinese-style spring rolls filled with finely shredded cabbage, carrot, mushrooms and aromatics.

method: Heat the neutral oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat.
Add the mushrooms and cook until their moisture has evaporated.
Add the cabbage and carrots.
Stir-fry for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened but still retaining some texture.
Add the bean sprouts, spring onions, garlic and ginger.
Stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the soy sauce, sesame oil, white pepper and rice vinegar.
Stir thoroughly.
Continue cooking until the filling is as dry as possible.
Transfer the filling to a large tray or bowl.
Spread it out and allow it to cool completely.
Mix the cornflour and water to make a thin paste.
Place one spring roll wrapper in front of you with one corner pointing towards you.
Place a small amount of filling across the lower third of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom corner over the filling.
Fold the side corners towards the centre.
Roll firmly towards the top corner.
Brush the final corner with the cornflour paste and seal.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat the oil to approximately 175°C.
Fry the spring rolls in batches until golden and crisp.
Drain on a wire rack.

learning.did-you-know: Spring rolls are associated with the Spring Festival and were traditionally connected with the arrival of spring and the first fresh vegetables of the season.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to create a dry filling suitable for a crisp pastry wrapper and how to roll and seal spring rolls for frying.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive, freezer-friendly vegetarian snack that works well alongside Asian meals and uses common vegetables and pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: The filling must be cooled completely before wrapping.
Cook the filling until it is as dry as possible.
Roll firmly but avoid tearing the wrapper.
Keep unused wrappers covered so they do not dry out.
Freeze uncooked spring rolls in a single layer before transferring them to a freezer bag.
Cook from frozen if the wrappers are suitable for it, allowing extra cooking time.
A wire rack helps keep the fried spring rolls crisp.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Freeze uncooked spring rolls in a single layer until solid, then transfer to a freezer bag or container.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in an oven or air fryer until hot and crisp.
